text.title: llm-friendly-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for llm-friendly-architecture as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: God component: a single file over 800 lines combining routing, authentication, data fetching, state management, calculations, and rendering.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Context truncation causes the agent to miss the lower half of the file entirely. Edits target the wrong section. Multiple concerns in one file means any change risks unintended side effects in other concerns.

failure-mode.description: Utils dumping ground: a utils/index.ts or helpers.ts with 50+ unrelated functions exported together.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents cannot infer which function to edit without reading the entire file. New functions get added to the dump rather than to purpose-named files. The file grows unboundedly.

failure-mode.description: Deep barrel re-exports: components/index.ts re-exporting from 20+ files with export * from './Button', export * from './Input', etc.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents see the barrel export and edit it as if it is the implementation. The actual Button.tsx is never opened. Circular dependency risk increases. After a large restructure, TypeScript path aliases may still resolve to old locations if tsconfig.json is not updated.

failure-mode.description: Inline large data: 200+ lines of JSON or data arrays defined inside a component function.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inflates component file past the point where the agent can hold the full rendering logic in context. Edits to data lines frequently corrupt adjacent rendering code.

failure-mode.description: Ambiguous file names: helpers.ts, utils.ts, common.ts, shared.ts, misc.ts used as catch-all files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents grep filenames to locate code. Ambiguous names produce multiple candidates; the agent opens the wrong file, makes the edit, and produces a no-op or a bug in the wrong module.
